symptoms.symptom_name: Partial bitemporal hemianopsia on confrontation visual field testing

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Optic chiasm compression by pituitary macroadenoma
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Bitemporal hemianopia localises to the optic chiasm — the hallmark of a supra-sellar pituitary mass. Formal field testing is a clinician measurement; the AI infers it from the patient-reported loss of side vision.

symptoms.symptom_name: Acute crescendo severe bilateral headache over 48 hours

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A new, escalating headache pattern in a suspected macroadenoma raises expanding mass or apoplexy.
A first, worst, progressive headache with a fixed field defect is atypical for migraine.

symptoms.symptom_name: New patient-reported loss of peripheral (side) vision in both eyes

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Optic chiasm compression by pituitary macroadenoma
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Patient-reportable proxy for bitemporal field loss; the telehealth-admissible signal that drives urgent escalation.

symptoms.symptom_name: Elevated serum IGF-1 / non-suppressed GH on OGTT

investigation_details.investigation_name: Serum IGF-1 and GH nadir on oral glucose tolerance test
investigation_details.expected_result: Elevated age-matched IGF-1 with failure of GH to suppress — confirms GH excess
investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: GH excess remains biochemically unconfirmed; acromegaly cannot be established

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Biochemical GH excess is the diagnostic anchor for acromegaly — a laboratory result never available in real time via telehealth.

symptoms.symptom_name: Progressive enlargement of hands and feet over ~2 years (larger ring/shoe sizes)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Acral overgrowth is a cardinal chronic feature of sustained GH/IGF-1 excess.

symptoms.symptom_name: Coarsening facies, prognathism, macroglossia, deepening voice, hyperhidrosis and oily skin

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Soft-tissue and skeletal overgrowth plus sweat/sebaceous overactivity form the classic acromegalic phenotype; facial coarsening and voice change are partly video-observable, formal features (prognathism, macroglossia, dental malocclusion) are clinician-confirmed.

symptoms.symptom_name: Severe large-joint arthropathy with restricted painful range of motion and proximal weakness

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Large-joint arthropathy and proximal myopathy are recognised complications of long-standing GH excess.
Large-joint, non-symmetrical, non-small-joint pattern without prolonged morning stiffness is not typical RA.
Hypertension is a common comorbidity of acromegaly but non-specific; a clinician-measured value not available via chat.

symptoms.symptom_name: Absence of thunderclap onset, vomiting or altered consciousness (apoplexy pertinent negatives)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Their absence makes established apoplexy less likely now, but does not exclude evolving apoplexy — hence aggressive safety-netting.

symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: Together the acral overgrowth, coarse facies/voice change and large-joint arthropathy make sustained GH excess the unifying chronic diagnosis.
New bitemporal field loss plus crescendo headache is the time-critical supra-sellar mass-effect picture threatening vision — the reason this is not a routine referral.

investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Defines the macroadenoma, supra-sellar extension, chiasm compression and any haemorrhage (apoplexy) — the decisive imaging study.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Formal visual field perimetry and ophthalmology assessment
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Quantifies and monitors the chiasmal field defect and helps determine urgency of decompression.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Serum IGF-1 and GH nadir on oral glucose tolerance test
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Confirms GH excess biochemically — the diagnostic anchor for acromegaly.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Anterior pituitary hormone profile (incl. cortisol, prolactin, TFTs, gonadotrophins)
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Screens for co-secretion and for hypopituitarism / secondary adrenal insufficiency that would need urgent steroid cover, especially if apoplexy supervenes.
Obsolete for pituitary assessment; MRI is the required modality and a skull X-ray would only delay definitive imaging.
